name: Berries with Orange-Honey Yogurt
recipeIngredient: Fresh mint leaves OR Thin strips of orange rind
(For those monitoring carbohydrates, consider reducing the amount of honey. Honey contains more carbohydrates than sugar.)
1/2 teaspoon Vanilla extract or almond extract
recipeInstructions:
Suggested fruit: Winter: sliced oranges, bananas, kiwi, pineapple, cantaloupe, or honeydew; Spring: strawberries, stewed rhubarb; Summer: raspberries, blackberries, blueberries; Fall: peaches, plums, grapes.
Combinations: Grapes & melon, melon & blueberries, blackberries & sliced peaches, raspberries & blueberries, strawberries & kiwi, bananas & sliced oranges.
1. Mix the sauce ingredients in a bowl.
2. Wash and hull the berries. If the fruit pieces are large, slice them.
3. Either mix the fruit with the sauce, cover and refrigerate for 1 hour, or at serving time, spoon the fruit into individual bowls or stemmed glasses and pour the sauce over it.
4. Garnish each serving with fresh mint leaves or thin strips of orange rind, and sprinkle toasted sliced almonds on top.
Diabetic caution: Honey has more carbohydrates than sugar. Consider reducing the amount of honey in the recipe if you are monitoring your carbohydrate intake.
